annotations.label: Rotation (mathematical)

anchor.snippet: a rotation is a rigid body movement which, unlike a translation

annotations.note: Mathlib defines rotation by an oriented angle in a 2D real inner product space (and via `Circle` on ℂ), but no general n-dimensional Euclidean rotation as such.

annotations.label: Classification of rigid body movements

anchor.snippet: All rigid body movements are rotations, translations, or combinations of the two.

annotations.note: No theorem in Mathlib classifying Euclidean isometries (rigid motions) as compositions of rotations and translations.

annotations.label: Rotations form a group

anchor.snippet: the rotations around a point/axis form a group

annotations.note: Mathlib has the special orthogonal group as a group and shows `Orientation.rotation_trans` / `rotation_trans` (Complex), but doesn't state the article's slogan as a stand-alone theorem.

annotations.label: Principal rotations

anchor.snippet: Rotations around the x , y and z axes are called principal rotations

annotations.note: Rotations about coordinate axes are not named/defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Decomposition into principal rotations

anchor.snippet: any spatial rotation can be decomposed into a combination of principal rotations

annotations.note: Euler-angle decomposition of an SO(3) element is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Composition of 3D rotations about a fixed point

anchor.snippet: The combination of any sequence of rotations of an object in three dimensions about a fixed point is always equivalent to a rotation about an axis

annotations.note: Euler's rotation theorem for SO(3) is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 2D rotations have no axis

anchor.snippet: 2-dimensional rotations, unlike the 3-dimensional ones, possess no axis of rotation

annotations.note: No Mathlib statement about absence of a fixed axis for 2D rotations.

annotations.label: No real eigenvector of a 2D rotation

anchor.snippet: no real vector in the plane is kept unchanged by A

annotations.note: Mathlib doesn't state that a nontrivial 2D rotation has no real fixed direction.

annotations.label: Rotation angle from trace

anchor.section: Rotation angle and axis in 3 dimensions
anchor.snippet: Knowing that the trace is an invariant

annotations.note: The trace–angle formula 1 + 2 cos θ for an SO(3) element is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of rotation axis in 3D

anchor.section: Rotation angle and axis in 3 dimensions
anchor.snippet: in 3D space has an axis of rotation

annotations.note: Mathlib does not prove that every element of SO(3) fixes a nontrivial axis.

annotations.label: Decomposition of improper orthogonal matrices

anchor.section: Rotation angle and axis in 3 dimensions
anchor.snippet: any improper orthogonal 3x3 matrix may be decomposed as a proper rotation

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks the decomposition O(3) = SO(3) × {±I-reflection}.

annotations.label: Existence of invariant rotation plane

anchor.snippet: every tridimensional rotation has a plane, which is perpendicular to the rotation axis

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the invariant plane perpendicular to the axis of a 3D rotation.

annotations.label: Rotating vector

anchor.snippet: A vector is said to be rotating if it changes its orientation

annotations.note: No definition of a time-varying rotating vector in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Perpendicular component characterizes rotation

anchor.snippet: a rotating vector always has a non-zero perpendicular component of its rate of change vector against the vector itself

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Moment of inertia (ratio)

anchor.snippet: The ratio of torque τ to the angular acceleration α is given by the moment of inertia

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize physical quantities like moment of inertia.

annotations.label: Euler's rotation theorem

anchor.snippet: any change in orientation can be described by rotation about an axis through a chosen reference point

annotations.note: Euler's rotation theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chasles' theorem

anchor.snippet: any motion of rigid bodies can be treated as a composition of rotation and translation

annotations.note: Chasles' theorem on rigid motions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler rotations

anchor.snippet: Euler rotations provide an alternative description of a rotation

annotations.note: Euler angle decomposition is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rotational invariance

anchor.snippet: A system which behaves the same regardless of how it is oriented in space is said to be rotationally invariant

annotations.note: No general definition of rotational invariance for physical systems in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Noether's theorem (rotation)

anchor.snippet: if the action (the integral over time of its Lagrangian) of a physical system is invariant under rotation, then angular momentum is conserved

annotations.note: Noether's theorem (Lagrangian/angular momentum conservation) is not in Mathlib.
